---
title: "The Chi Square test"
author: "Professor Andy Field"
institute: "University of Sussex"
format: 
  revealjs:
    auto-play-media: true
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: ../shared_media/images/slide_background_title.jpg
      data-background-size: 100%
      data-background-position: center
      data-background-opacity: "1"
execute: 
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
# general
library(easystats)
library(tidyverse)
# specific
# remotes::install_github("haleyjeppson/ggmosaic")
library(ggmosaic)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)


source("../helpers/discovr_helpers.R")


null_tib <- tibble::tibble(
  Organism = c(rep("Human", 50), rep("Zombie", 50)) |> 
    as_factor() |> 
    fct_relevel("Zombie"), 
  Chips = c(rep("Brain chips", 20), rep("Potato chips", 30), rep("Brain chips", 20), rep("Potato chips", 30))  |> as_factor()
  )

h1_tib <- tibble::tibble(
  Organism = c(rep("Human", 50), rep("Zombie", 50)) |> 
    as_factor()  |> 
    fct_relevel("Zombie"), 
  Chips = c(rep("Brain chips", 10), rep("Potato chips", 40), rep("Brain chips", 30), rep("Potato chips", 20))  |> as_factor()
  )

set.seed("7526")
n_zom <- round(runif(1, min = 50, max = 150))
n_hum <- round(runif(1, min = 50, max = 150))
bc_hum <- round(n_hum*1/5)
bc_zom <- round(n_zom*2/5)

brain_tib <- tibble::tibble(
  organism = c(rep("Human", n_hum), rep("Zombie", n_zom)) |> 
    as_factor()  |> 
    fct_relevel("Zombie"), 
  chips = c(rep("Brain chips", bc_hum), rep("Potato chips", n_hum-bc_hum), rep("Brain chips", bc_zom), rep("Potato chips", n_zom-bc_zom))  |> as_factor()
  )



```

## tl;dw

::: {.callout-note icon = false}
## {{< fa chart-line >}} Statis-tip

Use a chi-square test to:

::: incremental

- Test for an association between two categorical variables
  - H~1~: There is an association between the variables.
  - H~0~: There is **no** association between the variables. 
- Each observation must fall into only 1 combination of categories (independence)
- Data are frequencies
- A significant test statistic $\chi^2$
  - The observed frequencies are **inconsistent** with the null hypothesis being true
  - Conclude: the association between variables is not zero
- A non-significant test statistic $\chi^2$
  - The observed frequencies are **consistent** with the null hypothesis being true
  - Conclude: the association between variables could be zero
  
:::
:::


## Do zombie's like to eat brains?

- There is a common cultural belief that zombies like to eat brains
- If this is true we'd expect zombies to choose brains to eat more than other foods.
- We wouldn't expect this preference in non-zombies.
- A researcher counted how many humans and zombies choose brain chips or potato chips to accompany their dinner at the university canteen


::: {.callout-caution icon = false}
## {{< fa brain >}} Hypothesis

- H~1~: There is an association between the type of organism and the type of chips eaten.
- H~0~: There is **no** association between the type of organism and the type of chips eaten.
:::

## The Data

- Data are counts/frequencies
- Imagine we observed 50 humans and 50 zombies
- If there is no association, how many observations would we expect in each cell?


```{r}
#| label: tbl_empty

blk_tbl <- tibble::tibble(
  `Organism` = c("Human", "Zombie", "Total"),
  `Brain chips` = c("", "", ""),
  `Potato chips` = c("", "", ""),
  Total = c("50", "50", "100")
  )

display(blk_tbl)
```



## Expected values

- What about if 40 had brain chips and 60 Potato chips?

```{r}
#| label: tbl_empty2

blk_tbl2 <- tibble::tibble(
  `Organism` = c("Human", "Zombie", "Total"),
  `Brain chips` = c("", "", "40"),
  `Potato chips` = c("", "", "60"),
  Total = c("50", "50", "100")
  )

display(blk_tbl2)
```

```{r}
h0_ggm <- ggplot(data = null_tib) +
  geom_mosaic(aes(x = product(Organism, Chips), fill = Organism)) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_d(begin = 0.2, end = 0.9) +
  theme_minimal()

h1_ggm <- ggplot(data = h1_tib) +
  geom_mosaic(aes(x = product(Organism, Chips), fill = Organism)) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_d(begin = 0.2, end = 0.9) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Expected values (generally)

```{r}
#| label: tbl_gen

gen_tbl <- tibble::tibble(
  `Organism` = c("Human", "Zombie", "Total"),
  `Brain chips` = c("$\\frac{\\text{RT}_1 \\times \\text{CT}_1}{N}$", "$\\frac{\\text{RT}_1 \\times \\text{CT}_2}{N}$", "CT~1~"),
  `Potato chips` = c("$\\frac{\\text{RT}_2 \\times \\text{CT}_1}{N}$", "$\\frac{\\text{RT}_2 \\times \\text{CT}_2}{N}$", "CT~2~"),
  Total = c("RT~1~", "RT~2~", "$N$")
  )

kable(gen_tbl, escape = T, align = "c")
```



## Model (expected) values

- The expected values when there's no association (i.e. the null is true)

```{r}
#| label: tbl_h0

h0_tbl <- tibble::tibble(
  `Organism` = c("Human", "Zombie", "Total"),
  `Brain chips` = c("20", "20", "40"),
  `Potato chips` = c("30", "30", "60"),
  Total = c("50", "50", "100")
  )

display(h0_tbl)
```

::: center-h
::: fragment

```{r}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3.5

h0_ggm
```

:::
:::

## Observed values


```{r}
#| label: tbl_h1

h1_tbl <- tibble::tibble(
  `Organism` = c("Human", "Zombie", "Total"),
  `Brain chips` = c("10", "30", "40"),
  `Potato chips` = c("40", "20", "60"),
  Total = c("50", "50", "100")
  )

display(h1_tbl)
```


::: center-h
::: fragment

```{r}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3.5

h1_ggm
```

:::
:::

## The Chi-square test

$$
\chi^2 = \sum\frac{\left(\text{observed}_{ij} - \text{model}_{ij}\right)^2}{\text{model}_{ij}}
$$

::::: columns
:::: {.column width="50%"}
### Model

```{r}
kable(h0_tbl, align = "c")
h0_ggm
```
::::

:::: {.column width="50%"}
::: fragment
### Observed

```{r}
kable(h1_tbl, align = "c") |> 
  column_spec(2, background = c(turbo_2[1], turbo_2[2], ""), color = c("white", "white", "")) |> 
  column_spec(3, background = c(turbo_2[2], turbo_2[1], ""), color = c("white", "white", "")) 
h1_ggm
```
:::
::::
:::::

```{r}
#| label: brain_xtble

# create the table
brain_xtbl <- brain_tib |>
  mutate(
    organism = fct_relevel(organism, "Human") # order to match mosaic plot
  ) |> 
  data_tabulate(select = "organism", by = "chips", remove_na = TRUE)

brain_mos <- ggplot(data = brain_tib) +
  geom_mosaic(aes(x = product(organism, chips), fill = organism)) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_d(begin = 0.2, end = 0.9) +
  theme_minimal()

brain_chi <- brain_xtbl |> 
  as.table(simplify = TRUE) |> 
  chisq.test()

brain_pe <- model_parameters(brain_chi)
brainz <- get_residuals(brain_chi)
brain_exp <- get_predicted(brain_chi)

null2_tib <- tibble::tibble(
  organism = brain_tib$organism, 
  chips = c(rep("Brain chips", round(brain_exp[1])), rep("Potato chips", round(brain_exp[3])), rep("Brain chips", round(brain_exp[2])), rep("Potato chips", round(brain_exp[4])))  |> as_factor()
  )

null_mos <- ggplot(data = null2_tib) +
  geom_mosaic(aes(x = product(organism, chips), fill = organism)) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_d(begin = 0.2, end = 0.9) +
  theme_minimal()

null_xtbl <- null2_tib |>
  mutate(
    organism = fct_relevel(organism, "Human") # order to match mosaic plot
  ) |>
  data_tabulate(select = "organism", by = "chips", remove_na = TRUE)
```

## In reality

::: tbl_l

```{r}

real_tib <- tibble::tibble(
  `Organism` = c("Human", "Zombie", "Total"),
  `Brain chips` = c(bc_hum, bc_zom, bc_hum+bc_zom),
  `Potato chips` = c(n_hum-bc_hum, n_zom-bc_zom, (n_hum-bc_hum)+(n_zom-bc_zom)),
  Total = c(n_hum, n_zom, n_zom + n_hum)
  ) 

display(real_tib)
  
```

:::

## In reality

$$
\chi^2 = \sum\frac{\left(\text{observed}_{ij} - \text{model}_{ij}\right)^2}{\text{model}_{ij}}
$$

::::: columns
:::: {.column width="50%"}
### Observed

```{r}
kable(brain_xtbl, align = "c")
brain_mos
```
::::

:::: {.column width="50%"}
::: fragment
### Model

```{r}
kable(null_xtbl, align = "c")
null_mos
```
:::
::::
:::::


# The chi-square test using `r rproj()`

## Fitting models is [E.V.I.L.]{.txt_ong}

::: incremental

- [L]{.txt_ong}ook and [L]{.txt_ong}oad: get the data into `r rproj()`, process it, summarize it (look)
- [V]{.txt_ong}isualise: plot relevant information to understand the data/model
- [E]{.txt_ong}valuate: is the model any good? Are its assumptions met? Does it 'fit' the data?
- [I]{.txt_ong}nterpret: use the model to answer your question

:::

## [L]{.txt_ong}oad and [L]{.txt_ong}ook

```{r}
brain_tib |>
  DT::datatable(caption = 'Table 1: Zombie data',
                options = list(
                dom = 'tp',
                columnDefs = list(
                  list(className = 'dt-center', targets = 1:2)
                  ),
                pageLength = 10
  )
  )
```

![](../shared_media/images/l_hex.png){.absolute top=0 left=800 height="80"}

## [L]{.txt_ong}oad and [L]{.txt_ong}ook


```{r}
#| label: xtble
#| echo: true

# create the table
brain_xtbl <- brain_tib |>
  data_tabulate(select = "organism", by = "chips", remove_na = TRUE)

# format for rendering
display(brain_xtbl)
```

![](../shared_media/images/l_hex.png){.absolute top=0 left=800 height="80"}


## [V]{.txt_ong}isualize


```{r}
#| label: mosaic
#| echo: true

ggplot(data = brain_tib) +
  geom_mosaic(aes(x = product(organism, chips), fill = organism)) + 
  theme_minimal()

```

![](../shared_media/images/v_hex.png){.absolute top=0 left=800 height="80"}


## [I]{.txt_ong}nterpret


```{r}
#| label: chifit
#| echo: true

# fit model
brain_chi <- brain_xtbl |> 
  as.table(simplify = TRUE) |> 
  chisq.test()

# show results
model_parameters(brain_chi) |> 
  display()

```

![](../shared_media/images/i_hex.png){.absolute top=0 left=900 height="80"}


## [I]{.txt_ong}nterpret


```{r}
#| label: chiz
#| echo: true

get_residuals(brain_chi)  |> 
  display()
```

![](../shared_media/images/i_hex.png){.absolute top=0 left=900 height="80"}


## [I]{.txt_ong}nterpret


```{r}
#| label: or
#| echo: true

brain_xtbl |>
  as.table(simplify = TRUE) |> 
  oddsratio() |> 
  display()
```

![](../shared_media/images/i_hex.png){.absolute top=0 left=900 height="80"}



## [E]{.txt_mulberry}valuate assumptions

```{r}
#| label: expected
#| echo: true

get_predicted(brain_chi)  |> 
  display()
```

![](../shared_media/images/e_hex.png){.absolute top=0 left=800 height="80"}


# The Odds Ratio

## tl;dw

::: {.callout-note icon = false}
## {{< fa chart-line >}} Statis-tip

The odds ratio:

::: incremental

- The odds of an event in one group relative to a comparison group
  - The odds of recovery in those receiving surgery compared to those not receiving surgery
  - The odds of getting a six pack in those doing Tai-Chi walking compared to those that don't
- OR > 1: the odds of an event occurring are **greater** in the group of interest than in the comparison group.
- OR < 1: the odds of an event occurring are **smaller** in the group of interest than in the comparison group.
  
:::
:::

## The Data

- There is a common cultural belief that zombies like to eat brains
- If this is true we'd expect zombies to choose brains to eat more than other foods.
- A researcher counted how many humans and zombies choose brain chips or potato chips to accompany their dinner at the university canteen
- Data are counts/frequencies

## The odds of eating brain

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: tbl8

real_tib |> 
  knitr::kable(align = 'lccc') |> 
  kableExtra::row_spec(row = 3, background = mulberry, color = "white") |> 
  kableExtra::column_spec(4, background = mulberry, color = "white")
```

\

::::: columns
:::: {.column width="45%"}
::: fragment
::: txt_s
$$
\begin{aligned}
\text{odds}_\text{human eating brain} &= \frac{\text{Number eating brain}}{\text{Number eating potato}} \\
&= \frac{27}{106} \\
&= 0.255
\end{aligned}
$$
:::
:::
::::

:::: {.column width="10%"}
:::: 

:::: {.column width="45%"}
::: fragment
::: txt_s
$$
\begin{aligned}
\text{odds}_\text{zombie eating brain} &= \frac{\text{Number eating brain}}{\text{Number eating potato}} \\
&= \frac{36}{53} \\
&= 0.679
\end{aligned}
$$
:::
:::
::::
:::::





## The odds ratio

:::: columns
::: {.column width="45%"}
$$
\begin{aligned}
\text{odds ratio} &= \frac{\text{odds}_\text{human eating brain}}{\text{odds}_\text{zombie eating brain}} \\
&= \frac{0.255}{0.679} \\
&= 0.38
\end{aligned}
$$
:::

::: {.column width="10%"}
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.callout-note icon = false}
## {{< fa chart-line >}} Statis-tip

- The odds of a human eating brains are 0.38 times those of a zombie eating brains 

:::
:::
::::

\

::::: fragment
:::: columns
::: {.column width="45%"}
$$
\begin{aligned}
\text{odds ratio} &= \frac{\text{odds}_\text{zombie eating brain}}{\text{odds}_\text{human eating brain}} \\
&= \frac{0.679}{0.255} \\
&= 2.66
\end{aligned}
$$
:::

::: {.column width="10%"}
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.callout-note icon = false}
## {{< fa chart-line >}} Statis-tip

- The odds of a zombie eating brains are 2.66 times greater than the odds of a human eating brains 

:::
:::
::::
:::::
